sustainability.definition: Meeting present needs without harming future generations.
examples: Using solar and wind energy instead of fossil fuels
facts: UN has 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) for 2030
Sustainable cities reduce carbon footprint and energy consumption

3r.reduce: Minimize consumption and waste; buy products with less packaging
3r.reuse: Use items again instead of throwing them away; example: cloth bags, jars, old notebooks
3r.recycle: Convert waste into raw material for new products like paper, plastic, metal
facts: Recycling 1 ton of paper saves 17 trees and 7000 gallons of water
Recycling 1 ton of aluminum saves 10 tons of bauxite ore
Recycling 1 ton of plastic saves 7.4 cubic meters of landfill space
Recycling reduces greenhouse gas emissions and energy consumption
tips: Prefer products with recyclable packaging
Donate old items instead of throwing them away
Use cloth bags and reusable containers
activities: Sort plastic and paper at home before disposal
Create DIY projects from reused items

waste_management.wet_waste: Food scraps, fruits, vegetables. Can be composted into fertilizer or biogas.
waste_management.dry_waste: Plastic, paper, metal, glass. Can be recycled into new products.
waste_management.hazardous_waste: Chemicals, batteries, medical waste. Needs special disposal.
tips: Segregate wet and dry waste at home
Avoid single-use plastics
Dispose e-waste at authorized centers
Start composting organic kitchen waste
facts: Plastic takes 500+ years to decompose
Segregation of waste reduces landfill load by 30–40%
India produces 150,000 tons of plastic waste per year
Composting wet waste reduces methane emissions
Improper disposal of hazardous waste harms soil and water
activities: Organize a neighborhood plastic collection day
Start a compost pit at home
Sort plastic waste and reuse containers for storage
Create DIY crafts from old bottles and jars

plastic_reuse.definition: Ways to reuse plastic bottles and containers instead of throwing them away.
tips: Use bottles as plant containers or mini gardens
Create storage boxes for small items
Make bird feeders from bottles
Use bottles to water plants gradually
activities: Sort and clean plastic waste at home for reuse
Organize a local reuse workshop in your neighborhood
facts: Recycling 1 ton of plastic saves 7.4 cubic meters of landfill space
Properly reused plastic reduces greenhouse gas emissions
Plastic waste is a major contributor to ocean pollution

climate_change.definition: Long-term changes in temperature and weather due to greenhouse gases.
effects: Melting glaciers and polar ice caps
Extreme weather events like floods, droughts, cyclones
Decline in crop yields and water scarcity
solutions: Plant trees and maintain green cover
Reduce carbon footprint and use public transport
facts: Global temperature has risen by 1.2°C since pre-industrial era
CO₂ levels reached 419 ppm in 2023
Climate change affects agriculture, water resources, and human health
Renewable energy adoption can reduce global emissions by 70%

global_warming.definition: Increase in Earth's average temperature caused by carbon emissions.
effects: Rising sea levels and flooding of coastal areas
Extreme heat waves and forest fires
Increased frequency of hurricanes and storms
solutions: Use renewable energy like solar, wind, hydro
facts: Earth's average temperature has increased by 1.2°C since 1850
Arctic ice is declining at a rate of 13% per decade
Global warming accelerates extreme weather events

composting.definition: Turns organic waste like food scraps into natural fertilizer.
examples: Kitchen waste, garden leaves, fruit peels
activities: Start a compost pit at home
Use kitchen scraps to fertilize plants

tips: Turn off taps when not in use
Fix leaks immediately
Use drip irrigation for plants
Collect and reuse rainwater
Avoid overwatering gardens
Use water-efficient appliances
facts: Drip irrigation saves up to 50% water
Fixing leaks can save 10–20 liters/day per tap
Using water-efficient appliances reduces consumption by 30%
Rainwater harvesting can supplement 50–60% household water needs

tips: Switch off lights and appliances when not in use
Use LED bulbs instead of incandescent bulbs
Use energy-efficient appliances
Prefer natural light during the day
Unplug chargers and devices when not in use
Use solar panels for electricity generation
facts: LED bulbs save 80% electricity compared to incandescent bulbs
Energy-efficient appliances reduce household energy consumption by 20–30%
Turning off unused appliances reduces CO₂ emissions
Solar energy can meet 25–30% of household electricity needs in sunny regions

plastic_facts.definition: Plastic is a synthetic material used in packaging, containers, and other products.
problems: Takes more than 500 years to decompose
Leaches chemicals into soil and water
solutions: Use cloth bags and reusable containers
Support bans on plastic bags and straws
